articles.title: US strikes Iran targets for second time in three days
articles.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.
articles.titleZh: 美国三天内第二次袭击伊朗目标
articles.summaryZh: 敌对行动发生在美国和伊朗之间脆弱的停火期间，并且为结束三个月的战争而进行了旷日持久的谈判。

contentParagraphs.en: Just nowSarah Smith,North America editor, Washington DCandNardine SaadUS Navy via Getty ImagesThe strikes come amid a fragile ceasefire and protracted negotiations to end the war, which began with the US and Israel striking Iran on 28 FebThe US military has carried out new strikes on Iran, targeting a military site in Bandar Abbas, a strategic port city. US Central Command (Centcom) said its forces also shot down four Iranian one-way attack drones "that posed a threat around the Strait of Hormuz".Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it had retaliated by targeting a US air base, without providing details of the its location.Meanwhile, Kuwait's army said its air defences were intercepting "hostile missile and drone threats", but did not specify where they were coming from.The renewed hostilities threaten a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran.It is the second time in three days that the US has attacked targets in Iran - saying that the strikes were conducted in self defence. The site in Bandar Abbas was struck as it was about to launch a fifth drone, Centcom said. Iranian media reported that explosions were heard to the east of the city. Centcom described its actions as "measured, purely defensive, and intended to maintain the ceasefire".Iran condemned the strikes as "a grave violation of the ceasefire" and vowed that the Iranian government "will not leave any act of hostility unanswered."Earlier this week, the US confirmed a previous round of "self-defence" strikes on southern Iran on Monday in which they targeted Iranian missile sites and boats attempting to lay mines in the Strait, where thousands of commercial tanker ships are stranded as a result of the conflict.Centcom said those strikes were designed "to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".The US also imposed sanctions on the "Persian Gulf Strait Authority" – the Iranian body tasked with collecting payments from ships travelling through the Strait of Hormuz.Any ships that pay the authority could also be "exposed to the risk of sanctions", the US Treasury Department said in a statement.One-fifth of the world's liquefied natural gas and oil normally pass through the shipping channel, and its closure has impacted global fuel trade.Iran's foreign ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said on Monday that Tehran was collecting fees for "navigational services" and it would continue to manage traffic through the waterway.US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent described it as the "Iranian military's latest attempt to extort global maritime trade" and "proof" Iran is "desperate for cash".The IRGC also said on Tuesday that it had downed a US drone and fired at a fighter jet and another drone that entered Iranian airspace, but did not specify when. Protracted negotiations have been taking place to end the three-month war that has choked traffic in the Strait of Hormuz and caused global energy prices to shoot up.During a cabinet meeting on Wednesday, US President Donald Trump said that Iran is "negotiating on fumes", insisting that his war strategy will not be impacted by November's US midterm elections."Maybe we have to go back and finish it, maybe we don't," he said. During that meeting, the president also urged Gulf nations to sign on to the Abraham Accords to normalise relations with Israel.Israel launched the war against Iran alongside the US on 28 February and is also embroiled in a war with Iranian-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.Trump has threatened to resume a large-scale bombing campaign if Iran does not agree to his terms.While Trump struck an optimistic note over the weekend, saying that a peace deal with Iran had been "largely negotiated", by Wednesday's cabinet meeting, he said that the US is "not satisfied".He said Tehran was "very much intent" on reaching an agreement to end the conflict, but added "so far they haven't gotten there", repeating Washington's willingness to resume strikes if one is not reached.His remarks came after Iranian state TV reported what it said were details of a draft agreement, which included reopening the Strait of Hormuz and the withdrawal of US forces from the region.The White House branded the text a "complete fabrication".Both sides signalled progress had been made towards a deal late last week, prompting speculation an announcement was close.However, Tehran soon cautioned a deal was "not imminent" while Trump said he had instructed his negotiators "not to rush into" one.Speaking to reporters at a cabinet meeting on Wednesday, the US president said: "They just want to make a deal - I don't think they have a choice."Trump also said: "Iran is very much intent, they want very much to make a deal."So far, they haven't gotten there and we're not satisfied with it, but we will be. Either that or we'll have to just finish the job."More on this story
contentParagraphs.zh: 刚刚，华盛顿特区北美编辑莎拉·史密斯和纳丁·萨阿德美国海军通过盖蒂图片社报道，这次袭击是在脆弱的停火和结束战争的旷日持久的谈判中进行的。这场战争始于美国和以色列于 2 月 28 日袭击伊朗。美国军方对伊朗进行了新的袭击，目标是战略港口城市阿巴斯港的一个军事基地。美国中央司令部（Centcom）表示，其部队还击落了四架伊朗单向攻击无人机，“对霍尔木兹海峡周围构成威胁”。伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队（IRGC）表示，它以美国空军基地为目标进行报复，但没有提供其具体位置。与此同时，科威特军队表示，其防空系统正在拦截“敌对导弹和无人机威胁”，但没有具体说明它们来自何处。新一轮的敌对行动威胁着脆弱的伊朗。美国和伊朗之间停火。这是三天内第二次袭击伊朗目标，并称袭击是出于自卫。中央司令部表示，阿巴斯港的基地在准备发射第五架无人机时遭到袭击。伊朗媒体报道称，该市东部听到爆炸声。中央司令部将其行动描述为“有节制的、纯粹防御性的、旨在维持停火”。伊朗谴责此次袭击“严重违反停火协议”，并誓言伊朗政府“不会对任何敌对行为置之不理。”本周早些时候，美国确认周一对伊朗南部进行了上一轮“自卫”袭击，袭击目标是伊朗导弹基地和试图在海峡埋雷的船只，数千艘商业油轮被困在该海峡。美国中央司令部表示，这些袭击的目的是“保护我们的部队免受伊朗军队的威胁”。美国还对“波斯湾海峡管理局”实施制裁，该机构的任务是向通过霍尔木兹海峡的船只收取费用。美国财政部在一份声明中表示，任何向该机构付款的船只也可能“面临制裁的风险”。世界上五分之一的液化天然气和石油通常通过海运伊朗外交部发言人埃斯梅尔·巴卡伊周一表示，德黑兰正在收取“导航服务”费用，并将继续管理该航道的交通。美国财政部长斯科特·贝森特称这是“伊朗军方勒索全球海上贸易的最新企图”，并“证明”伊朗“急需现金”。伊朗革命卫队周二还表示，击落了一架美国无人机和向进入伊朗领空的一架战斗机和另一架无人机开火，但没有具体说明具体时间。为了结束三个月的战争，这场战争导致霍尔木兹海峡交通堵塞，并导致全球能源价格飙升。在周三的内阁会议上，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普表示，伊朗正在进行“毫无硝烟的谈判”，并坚称他的战争战略不会受到11月美国中期选举的影响。“也许我们必须回去完成它，也许我们不需要，”他说。在那次会议上，总统还敦促海湾国家签署《亚伯拉罕协议》，实现与以色列关系正常化。以色列于 2 月 28 日与美国一起对伊朗发动了战争，并卷入了与伊朗支持的黎巴嫩真主党的战争。特朗普威胁称，如果伊朗不同意他的条件，他将恢复大规模轰炸行动。虽然特朗普在周末发表了乐观的言论，称与伊朗的和平协议已经“基本通过谈判”，但在周三的内阁会议上，他表示美国“不满意”。他表示德黑兰“非常愿意”达成结束冲突的协议，但补充说“到目前为止他们还没有实现这一目标”，重申华盛顿愿意在达不成协议的情况下恢复打击。他发表此番言论之前，伊朗国家电视台报道了一份协议草案的细节，其中包括重新开放霍尔木兹海峡以及美军从该地区撤军。白宫称该文本是“完整的”双方均表示上周晚些时候在达成协议方面取得了进展，引发人们猜测宣布协议已接近完成。然而，德黑兰很快警告称，协议“不会很快达成”，而特朗普则表示，他已指示谈判代表“不要仓促达成”协议。美国总统在周三的内阁会议上对记者表示：“他们只是想达成协议 - 我认为他们别无选择。” 特朗普还表示：“伊朗非常有意愿，他们非常想要达成协议“到目前为止，他们还没有做到这一点，我们对此并不满意，但我们会的。要么这样，要么我们就必须完成这项工作。”更多关于这个故事的信息

articles.titleZh: 以色列在撤离令后袭击黎巴嫩南部

articles.title: Iranians are back online after a monthslong shutdown but face heavy restrictions
articles.summary: Iranians began to regain internet access after authorities ended a monthslong shutdown. Users said service was slow and spotty in some areas, with apps like YouTube and Instagram heavily restricted.
articles.titleZh: 伊朗人在关闭一个月后重新上线，但面临严格限制
articles.summaryZh: 当局结束长达数月的封锁后，伊朗人开始重新获得互联网接入。用户表示，某些地区的服务速度缓慢且不稳定，YouTube 和 Instagram 等应用程序受到严格限制。

contentParagraphs.en: A woman checks her smartphone while sitting on a bench along a sidewalk in northern Tehran, Iran, Tuesday, May 26, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 5 月 26 日星期二，伊朗德黑兰北部，一名妇女坐在人行道上的长凳上检查她的智能手机。

contentParagraphs.en: CAIRO — Iranians began to regain internet access on Wednesday after authorities ended a monthslong shutdown. But users said service was slow and spotty in some areas, with apps like YouTube and Instagram heavily restricted, as they were before the cutoff began during nationwide protests in January.
contentParagraphs.zh: 开罗——在当局结束长达数月的封锁后，伊朗人于周三开始重新获得互联网接入。但用户表示，某些地区的服务速度缓慢且不稳定，YouTube 和 Instagram 等应用程序受到严格限制，就像 1 月份全国抗议期间服务中断之前一样。

contentParagraphs.en: Authorities justified the outage as a military imperative after the United States and Israel attacked Iran on Feb. 28. Their decision to lift some restrictions this week came as negotiators appeared to be closing in on a more permanent truce. But many Iranians feared access could be cut off again at a moment's notice.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2 月 28 日美国和以色列袭击伊朗后，当局将此次停电视为军事需要。他们本周决定取消部分限制，此时谈判代表似乎正在接近达成更永久的休战协议。但许多伊朗人担心，访问可能会在接到通知后再次被切断。

contentParagraphs.en: Internet tracking company Netblocks said Iran's connectivity, which measures the ability of devices to connect to the internet, is at around 86% of capacity from before the cutoff. Internet analysis firm Kentik said internet traffic, which measures the amount of data transferred and is a good illustration of usage, was at around 40%.
contentParagraphs.zh: 互联网跟踪公司 Netblocks 表示，伊朗的连接能力（衡量设备连接互联网的能力）目前约为断电前的 86%。互联网分析公司 Kentik 表示，互联网流量（衡量传输的数据量并很好地说明了使用情况）约为 40%。

contentParagraphs.en: Amir Rashidi, an Iranian cybersecurity analyst, said there were still widespread disruptions. "It's too early to say the shutdown is over," he wrote on X.
contentParagraphs.zh: 伊朗网络安全分析师阿米尔·拉希迪表示，仍然存在广泛的干扰。他在 X 上写道：“现在说关闭已经结束还为时过早。”

contentParagraphs.en: An unprecedented shutdown
contentParagraphs.zh: 史无前例的停工

contentParagraphs.en: Iran's roughly 90 million people have been cut off from the internet for most of 2026, one of the world's longest and strictest national shutdowns. Young people with online careers saw their incomes evaporate. Job losses and the closure of online businesses added to the war's steep economic costs.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年的大部分时间里，伊朗约有 9000 万人无法使用互联网，这是世界上封锁时间最长、最严格的国家之一。从事在线职业的年轻人的收入蒸发了。失业和在线企业倒闭加剧了战争造成的高昂经济成本。

contentParagraphs.en: The cutoff made it difficult for Iranian families to communicate through months of unrest and war. At some points, phone lines were also cut off, though they were later restored.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在数月的动乱和战争中，切断联系使伊朗家庭难以沟通。在某些时候，电话线也被切断，但后来又恢复了。

contentParagraphs.en: A woman living in Tehran said that for months she was barely able to speak to her sons living abroad. She couldn't believe authorities had restored access, saying she had assumed they would find some justification to prolong the outage.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一位住在德黑兰的妇女说，几个月来她几乎无法与住在国外的儿子们说话。她不敢相信当局已经恢复了访问，并表示她以为他们会找到一些理由来延长停电时间。

contentParagraphs.en: A taxi driver said service was restored but weak. He expressed hope it would improve so he could use messaging apps with family and friends. Both spoke on condition of anonymity for security reasons.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一名出租车司机表示，服务已经恢复，但很弱。他表示希望情况能有所改善，这样他就可以与家人和朋友使用消息应用程序。出于安全原因，两人都要求匿名。

contentParagraphs.en: Prices spiked during the shutdown, with residents in Tehran at times paying around $7.50 per gigabyte. Prices are back down to around $2.25 for 30 gigabytes, roughly where they were before the protests.
contentParagraphs.zh: 关闭期间价格飙升，德黑兰居民有时每 GB 支付 7.5 美元左右。价格回落至 30 GB 2.25 美元左右，大致与抗议前的水平相同。

contentParagraphs.en: Even then, Iran tightly controlled access to popular social media sites, leading many to rely on virtual private networks, or VPNs. The cost of those workarounds soared during the shutdown, making them unaffordable for many as the economy was battered.
contentParagraphs.zh: 即便如此，伊朗仍严格控制对流行社交媒体网站的访问，导致许多人依赖虚拟专用网络（VPN）。在政府关门期间，这些解决办法的成本飙升，在经济遭受重创的情况下，许多人都无力承担。

contentParagraphs.en: A slow return to service
contentParagraphs.zh: 缓慢恢复服务

contentParagraphs.en: Businesses have started reappearing online, announcing their return with posts on sites like Instagram and Telegram.
contentParagraphs.zh: 企业已开始重新出现在网上，并在 Instagram 和 Telegram 等网站上发布帖子宣布回归。

contentParagraphs.en: A gamer and tech influencer in the central city of Isfahan said the shutdown had caused him to lose a lot of his audience on YouTube and Instagram, where he had spent years building up a large following.
contentParagraphs.zh: 中部城市伊斯法罕的一位游戏玩家和科技影响者表示，政府关闭导致他在 YouTube 和 Instagram 上失去了很多观众，而他多年来在 YouTube 和 Instagram 上积累了大批粉丝。

contentParagraphs.en: "All my views and interactions are way down. I've been erased from the algorithm," he said in a voice note sent by WhatsApp, adding that his internet connection was still slower than before the shutdown.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他在 WhatsApp 发送的语音留言中说：“我所有的观点和互动都大幅下降。我已被从算法中删除。”他补充说，他的互联网连接仍然比关闭前慢。

contentParagraphs.en: "The situation is such that many content producers have had their income reduced to zero, have moved on to other jobs, or have been forced to sell their equipment to survive," he said. He spoke on condition of anonymity for fear of reprisal.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说：“目前的情况是，许多内容制作者的收入已降至零，要么转向其他工作，要么被迫出售设备以求生存。”由于担心遭到报复，他要求匿名。

contentParagraphs.en: Iran claimed the shutdown was a wartime necessity
contentParagraphs.zh: 伊朗声称关闭是战时必要的

contentParagraphs.en: Iranian authorities first shut down the internet in January during mass anti-government protests that were eventually stamped out in a violent crackdown. Thousands of people were killed and tens of thousands detained.
contentParagraphs.zh: 伊朗当局在一月份大规模反政府抗议活动期间首次关闭了互联网，抗议活动最终在暴力镇压中被平息。数千人被杀，数万人被拘留。

contentParagraphs.en: That cutoff was just starting to ease when the government imposed a complete internet blackout after the start of the war, when U.S. and Israeli strikes killed Iran's supreme leader and other top officials.
contentParagraphs.zh: 战争开始后，当美国和以色列的袭击杀死了伊朗最高领导人和其他高级官员时，政府实施了全面的互联网封锁，这种封锁才刚刚开始缓解。

contentParagraphs.en: The government faced criticism for the prolonged shutdown, which caused even more harm to an economy devastated by inflation, strikes on key industries and a U.S. blockade on Iranian ports.
contentParagraphs.zh: 政府因长期关闭而面临批评，这对因通货膨胀、关键行业罢工和美国封锁伊朗港口而遭受重创的经济造成了更大的伤害。

contentParagraphs.en: The internet cutoff cost an estimated $30-40 million daily, with indirect losses likely twice that much, a member of Iran's Chamber of Commerce, Afshin Kolahi, told a local newspaper last month. About 10 million people have jobs that depend on internet connectivity, according to Communications Minister Sattar Hashemi.
contentParagraphs.zh: 伊朗商会成员阿夫辛·科拉希 (Afshin Kolahi) 上个月对当地一家报纸表示，互联网中断每天造成的损失估计为 30-4000 万美元，间接损失可能是这个数字的两倍。据通讯部长萨塔尔·哈希米 (Sattar Hashemi) 称，大约有 1000 万人的工作依赖于互联网连接。

contentParagraphs.en: Iranians still had access to a national net, but that has a far narrower reach, and users complained of poor service and heavy censorship. Senior government officials are given SIM cards granting them access to the global internet. Under pressure, the government expanded access to the SIM cards to some professions during the shutdown.
contentParagraphs.zh: 伊朗人仍然可以访问全国网络，但覆盖范围要小得多，用户抱怨服务质量差和审查制度严格。政府高级官员获得 SIM 卡，可以访问全球互联网。迫于压力，政府在停工期间将 SIM 卡的使用范围扩大到了一些职业。

articles.title: Happy, who taught researchers that elephants can recognize themselves, is euthanized
articles.summary: Happy, a Bronx Zoo elephant who gave researchers new insight into the animal's behavior and became the crux of a closely watched animal rights case, has been euthanized at age 55.
articles.titleZh: 哈皮（Happy）曾教导研究人员大象可以认出自己，却被安乐死
articles.summaryZh: 布朗克斯动物园的大象哈皮 (Happy) 在 55 岁时被安乐死，它让研究人员对动物的行为有了新的认识，并成为备受关注的动物权利案件的关键。

contentParagraphs.en: Bronx Zoo elephant "Happy" strolls inside the zoo's Asia Habitat in New York, Oct. 2, 2018.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2018 年 10 月 2 日，纽约布朗克斯动物园的大象“快乐”在动物园的亚洲栖息地内漫步。

contentParagraphs.en: NEW YORK — Happy, a Bronx Zoo elephant who gave researchers new insight into the animal's behavior and became the crux of a closely watched animal rights case, has been euthanized at age 55, the zoo said Wednesday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 纽约——布朗克斯动物园的一头大象哈皮 (Happy) 周三表示，它已于 55 岁被安乐死，它让研究人员对动物的行为有了新的认识，并成为备受关注的动物权利案件的关键。

contentParagraphs.en: The Asian elephant was put to sleep Tuesday at the zoo where she lived for almost a half-century. Zoo officials said some age-related conditions accelerated in recent weeks, and she showed signs of a falloff in kidney or liver function. A necropsy revealed arthritis and large, inoperable uterine tumors that are impossible to diagnose in elephants through exams or imaging, the zoo said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周二，这头亚洲象在她生活了近半个世纪的动物园里被安乐死。动物园官员表示，最近几周，一些与年龄相关的病症加速了，她出现了肾功能或肝功能下降的迹象。动物园表示，尸检显示大象患有关节炎和巨大的、无法手术的子宫肿瘤，这些肿瘤不可能通过检查或成像来诊断。

contentParagraphs.en: "She was a wonderful elephant," interim zoo director Craig Piper said in an interview Wednesday, as heavy-hearted staffers absorbed the loss of an animal some had tended for over 30 years. "She served as a tremendous ambassador for elephants and for elephant conservation."
contentParagraphs.zh: 动物园临时园长克雷格·派珀 (Craig Piper) 在周三接受采访时表示：“她是一头很棒的大象。”工作人员心情沉重，正在承受失去这只大象的痛苦，有些人已经照顾了 30 多年。 “她是大象和大象保护的杰出大使。”

contentParagraphs.en: Since Happy's death, the zoo's 57-year-old Patty is the last elephant on exhibit in the United States' largest city. The zoo's parent institution, the Wildlife Conservation Society, decided 20 years ago to stop acquiring pachyderms.
contentParagraphs.zh: 哈皮去世后，动物园 57 岁的帕蒂是美国最大城市展出的最后一头大象。该动物园的上级机构野生动物保护协会 20 年前决定停止收购厚皮类动物。

contentParagraphs.en: Born in the wild in Asia, Happy was brought to the U.S. as a 1-year-old. She was named for a character from "Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs" before arriving at the zoo in 1977.
contentParagraphs.zh: Happy 出生在亚洲的野外，一岁时被带到美国。她于 1977 年来到动物园之前，以《白雪公主和七个小矮人》中的一个角色命名。

contentParagraphs.en: Happy keenly engaged with her keepers and was easy to motivate with favorite treats, such as watermelon or strawberries, said Keith Lovett, the zoo's director of animal programs. Piper said she sometimes stashed treats in her ear to save for later.
contentParagraphs.zh: 动物园动物项目主任基思·洛维特 (Keith Lovett) 表示，哈皮与饲养员的互动非常积极，而且很容易通过西瓜或草莓等最喜欢的食物来激励它。派珀说，她有时会在耳边藏一些零食以备后用。

contentParagraphs.en: In 2005, she showed researchers that elephants can recognize themselves in a mirror — a sign of self-awareness seen in only a few other species. During the experiment, Happy faced her reflection and repeatedly used her trunk to touch an "X" painted above her eye, a mark she could see only in the mirror.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2005年，她向研究人员展示大象可以在镜子中认出自己——这是只有少数其他物种才能看到的自我意识的标志。在实验过程中，哈皮面对着自己的倒影，反复用她的鼻子触摸她眼睛上方画的“X”，这是她只能在镜子中看到的标记。

contentParagraphs.en: She was paired with other elephants until her last partner died in 2006. Happy then lived separately from Patty and a third elephant out of concern that they wouldn't get along, though Lovett said the animals could see, smell and touch each other over a divider. The third elephant, called Maxine, died in 2018.
contentParagraphs.zh: 她一直与其他大象配对，直到她的最后一个伙伴于 2006 年去世。随后，哈皮与帕蒂和第三头大象分开居住，因为担心它们无法相处，尽管洛维特说这些动物可以通过隔板看到、闻到和触摸彼此。第三头大象名叫玛克辛（Maxine），于 2018 年去世。

contentParagraphs.en: Zoo officials said the median life expectancy for Asian elephants in U.S. zoos is about 45 years. Their life expectancy in the wild is more difficult to pinpoint.
contentParagraphs.zh: 动物园官员表示，美国动物园中亚洲象的平均预期寿命约为 45 岁。它们在野外的预期寿命更难以确定。

contentParagraphs.en: During Happy's lifetime, zoo elephant exhibits came under increased scrutiny. Some experts said urban animal parks were too small for creatures that roam extensive distances in the wild. Animal rights activists argued that zoo enclosures were no place for big-brained, social pachyderms.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在哈皮的一生中，动物园的大象展览受到了越来越严格的审查。一些专家表示，城市动物园对于在野外漫游的动物来说太小了。动物权利活动人士认为，动物园围栏不适合大大脑、群居性厚皮动物。

contentParagraphs.en: Some zoos phased out their exhibits and sent elephants to sanctuaries, though some other zoos remain committed to keeping and breeding the creatures, arguing that they help keep people interested in saving wildlife.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些动物园逐步取消了展览，并将大象送往保护区，但其他一些动物园仍然致力于饲养和繁殖这些动物，认为它们有助于保持人们对拯救野生动物的兴趣。

contentParagraphs.en: One activist group, the Nonhuman Rights Project, sued the Bronx Zoo in 2018, seeking to have Happy declared a "person" for legal purposes and moved to a large animal sanctuary. It was the first such case about an elephant, according to the group.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一个名为“非人权项目”的活动组织于 2018 年起诉布朗克斯动物园，寻求让哈皮出于合法目的宣布为“人”，并搬到一个大型动物保护区。据该组织称，这是第一起有关大象的此类案件。

contentParagraphs.en: Citing a principle that's used to challenge the legality of a person's imprisonment, the activist group said Happy was "an extraordinarily cognitively complex and autonomous nonhuman being" who was unlawfully deprived of her liberty and suffered from being pent up in a exhibit without other elephants.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该活动组织引用了一项用于质疑监禁合法性的原则，称哈皮是“一个认知极其复杂且具有自主性的非人类”，她被非法剥夺了自由，并因被关在没有其他大象的展览中而遭受痛苦。

contentParagraphs.en: Zoo officials said Happy was assiduously cared for and had space for swimming, foraging and other natural behavior. Uprooting her from her longtime home could harm her, the zoo said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 动物园官员表示，哈皮受到精心照顾，并有空间进行游泳、觅食和其他自然行为。动物园表示，将她从长期居住的家中赶走可能会伤害她。

contentParagraphs.en: New York's top court ultimately rejected the activists' claim, by a 5-2 majority. Colorado's highest court later issued a similar ruling about five elephants in a zoo there.
contentParagraphs.zh: 纽约最高法院最终以 5 比 2 的多数驳回了活动人士的诉讼请求。科罗拉多州最高法院后来对该动物园的五头大象做出了类似的裁决。

contentParagraphs.en: Still, two of the New York high court judges wrote pointed dissents. One called Happy's captivity "inherently unjust and inhumane" and "an affront to a civilized society."
contentParagraphs.zh: 尽管如此，纽约高等法院的两名法官仍然提出了尖锐的异议。有人称哈皮的囚禁“本质上是不公正和不人道的”，是“对文明社会的侮辱”。

contentParagraphs.en: The Nonhuman Rights Project has continued pursuing cases about elephants in various other states.
contentParagraphs.zh: 非人权项目继续在其他各州追查有关大象的案件。

contentParagraphs.en: Happy spent her final weeks, by her choice, in an off-exhibit barn and yard within her enclosure, Piper said. In a zoo version of hospice care, staffers provided hydration, nutrition and pain management, he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 派珀说，根据她的选择，哈皮在她的围栏内的一个非展览谷仓和院子里度过了她的最后几周。他说，在动物园版的临终关怀中，工作人员提供水合作用、营养和疼痛管理。

contentParagraphs.en: Meanwhile, Patty is doing well, the zoo said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 动物园表示，与此同时，帕蒂状况良好。

contentParagraphs.en: The Wildlife Conservation Society said in 2006 that once there was only one elephant, the animal might be moved to another zoo if circumstances were right. Piper said the zoo will be "really thoughtful and careful" in contemplating whether to move Patty from her home of 53 years.
contentParagraphs.zh: 野生动物保护协会在2006年表示，一旦只有一头大象，如果情况合适，它可能会被转移到另一个动物园。派珀表示，动物园在考虑是否将帕蒂从她居住了 53 年的家搬走时，将会“非常周到和谨慎”。

articles.title: Ex-CIA official charged with stealing millions of dollars in gold bars
articles.summary: A former senior CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing hundreds of gold bars worth more than $40 million from the federal government and stashing them in his home.
articles.titleZh: 前中央情报局官员被控盗窃数百万美元的金条
articles.summaryZh: 一名拥有绝密级别权限的前中央情报局高级官员被指控从联邦政府窃取了数百根价值超过 4000 万美元的金条，并将其藏在家中。

contentParagraphs.en: The seal of the Central Intelligence Agency is displayed at CIA headquarters in Langley, Va., April 13, 2016.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2016 年 4 月 13 日，中央情报局的印章陈列在弗吉尼亚州兰利的中央情报局总部。

contentParagraphs.en: A former senior CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing hundreds of gold bars worth more than $40 million from the federal government and stashing them in his home.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一名拥有绝密级别权限的前中央情报局高级官员被指控从联邦政府窃取了数百根价值超过 4000 万美元的金条，并将其藏在家中。

contentParagraphs.en: David Rush was arrested and charged with criminal theft of public money last week, according to federal court filings in Virginia, where he lives.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据戴维·拉什所居住的弗吉尼亚州联邦法院的文件，他上周被捕并被指控盗窃公共资金。

contentParagraphs.en: From November to March, Rush requested and received a "significant quantity of foreign currency and tens of millions of dollars in gold bars for work-related expenses," according to an affidavit from an FBI agent investigating the case.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据调查此案的联邦调查局特工的一份证词，从 11 月到 3 月，拉什要求并收到“大量外币和数千万美元的金条，用于与工作相关的费用”。

contentParagraphs.en: The affidavit says its unclear what Rush intended to use the funds for, but that a portion of it was found in a storage space near his office.
contentParagraphs.zh: 证词称，目前尚不清楚拉什打算将这些资金用于什么用途，但其中一部分是在他办公室附近的储藏室中发现的。

contentParagraphs.en: Federal officials searched his home on May 18 and seized more than 300 gold bars with an estimated value of more than $40 million, according to the affidavit. They also seized roughly $2 million in U.S. currency and some 35 luxury watches, many of them Rolexes. Rush was arrested the next day, the FBI said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 证词称，联邦官员于 5 月 18 日搜查了他的家，查获了 300 多根金条，估计价值超过 4000 万美元。他们还查获了约 200 万美元的美元和约 35 块豪华手表，其中许多是劳力士手表。联邦调查局称，拉什第二天被捕。

contentParagraphs.en: The FBI affidavit concludes that there's probable cause to believe that Rush "knowingly embezzled, stole, purloined, or knowingly converted a thing of value of the United States" for his personal use.
contentParagraphs.zh: 联邦调查局宣誓书的结论是，有可能有理由相信拉什“故意贪污、偷窃、盗窃或故意转换美国有价值的物品”供个人使用。

contentParagraphs.en: The FBI said it was working with the CIA and Department of Justice in its investigation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 联邦调查局表示正在与中央情报局和司法部合作进行调查。

contentParagraphs.en: Rush's lawyer declined to comment Wednesday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 拉什的律师周三拒绝发表评论。

contentParagraphs.en: It's not clear what role Rush had at the CIA or when he left the agency. He's described in court filings simply as a "former senior executive service-level employee at a United States government agency." Spokespersons for the FBI declined to comment further, and the CIA didn't respond to an email.
contentParagraphs.zh: 目前尚不清楚拉什在中央情报局担任什么职务，也不清楚他何时离开该机构。在法庭文件中，他被简单地描述为“美国政府机构的前高级管理服务级雇员”。联邦调查局发言人拒绝进一步置评，中央情报局也没有回复电子邮件。

contentParagraphs.en: The FBI affidavit also notes that Rush appears to have lied for years about his education and military background. The bureau's investigation found he had falsely claimed to be a Navy pilot and that he'd graduated from Clemson University in South Carolina and Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute in New York.
contentParagraphs.zh: 联邦调查局的证词还指出，拉什似乎多年来一直在其教育和军事背景方面撒谎。该局的调查发现，他谎称自己是一名海军飞行员，并毕业于南卡罗来纳州克莱姆森大学和纽约伦斯勒理工学院。

contentParagraphs.en: Instead, they found he had enlisted in the Navy in 1997 and then served in the U.S. Navy Reserves from 2004 until 2015, when he was honorably discharged as a lieutenant. The affidavit states he does not appear to have underwent any evaluations as a pilot during that time, and he did not attend either college.
contentParagraphs.zh: 相反，他们发现他于 1997 年入伍，然后从 2004 年到 2015 年在美国海军预备役服役，并于 2015 年以中尉身份光荣退役。宣誓书称，在那段时间里，他似乎没有接受过任何作为飞行员的评估，也没有上过任何一所大学。

contentParagraphs.en: Rush is being held in custody pending a hearing Friday in federal court in Alexandria, Virginia.
contentParagraphs.zh: 拉什目前被拘留，等待周五在弗吉尼亚州亚历山大联邦法院举行的听证会。

articles.title: Ebola outbreak: What travel restrictions have countries imposed?
articles.summary: Some countries ban arrivals from affected countries while others step up screening to contain rare strain.
articles.titleZh: 埃博拉疫情：各国实施了哪些旅行限制？
articles.summaryZh: 一些国家禁止来自受影响国家的入境，而另一些国家则加强筛查以遏制罕见病毒。

contentParagraphs.en: The latest outbreak of a rare strain of the Ebola virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda has prompted several governments to take action in a bid to stop the spread of the disease.The World Health Organization (WHO) has recorded 220 suspected deaths and 900 suspected cases of the lethal Bundibugyo (BVD) strain of Ebola in DRC since Kinshasa declared the outbreak on May 15. In Uganda, five cases and one death have been confirmed.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Attacks on Ebola centres intensify in eastern DRC amid outbreak fearslist 2 of 3Ebola spreads in the DRC as authorities struggle to contain outbreaklist 3 of 3How did a rare strain of Ebola outrun doctors?end of listThe United Nations health agency raised its risk assessment from high to very high at the national level for DRC last week, but continues to assess the risk as low at the global level.Nevertheless, several countries have announced travel bans and temporary border measures to contain the spread of the new strain.Efforts to contain the virus in affected countriesThis week, the Congolese Ministry of Transport and Communications suspended all flights to and from Bunia in eastern DRC in an attempt to contain the Ebola outbreak. The Bunia health zone is one of 11 DRC health zones affected by the disease. Some exceptions, such as humanitarian, medical and emergency flights, may be allowed with special approval from aviation and health authorities.Uganda has also introduced restrictions on travel to and from the DRC. All direct flights have been suspended, while bus and boat border crossings have been halted for four weeks. Weekly markets in border districts have been put on hold. Freight traffic, essential goods and food supplies, however, are still permitted to cross.Countries imposing travel bansBeyond the immediate affected region, Canada and the Bahamas said they would temporarily ban residents of the DRC, Uganda and South Sudan from entering.Residents from those countries will be unable to travel to Canada for 90 days from Wednesday, the government said. Canadian citizens, permanent residents and other foreign nationals who have been in affected areas in recent weeks must quarantine for 21 days from May 30, even if they do not show symptoms, Canada’s public health agency said.The Bahamian government said entry restrictions would take immediate effect and remain in place for a period of 30 days, subject to review by the Caribbean country’s health ministry.Last week, the United States banned all non-citizens who had travelled to the DRC, Uganda or South Sudan in the previous 21 days from entering the country. On Friday, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) extended the ban to green card holders who have been in those countries in the previous 21 days.US citizens who have travelled to affected countries have been told to return to the US via selected airports equipped with enhanced screening. These are Washington Dulles International Airport (IAD) for flights to the US departing after May 21, 2026; Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport (ATL) for flights after May 22; and George Bush Intercontinental Airport, (IAH), Houston, for flights departing after May 26.The Wall Street Journal has reported that the Trump administration is expected to deploy US public ⁠health officers to ⁠Kenya to staff a potential quarantine facility, intended for Americans who have been exposed to or are at high ⁠risk of testing positive for the virus in the region, as well as for those who have already tested positive.Jordan suspended the entry of people arriving from the DRC and Uganda on May 19, according to the Jordanian state news agency. The same day, Bahrain, too, suspended the entry of foreign travellers arriving from South Sudan, the DRC and Uganda for 30 days.No Ebola cases have been recorded in Canada, the Bahamas, the US, Jordan or Bahrain.Countries stepping up screeningIndia has set up additional screening measures at major international airports, apart from issuing travel advisories asking its citizens to avoid visiting the DRC, Uganda and South Sudan.New Delhi has also postponed an India-Africa summit it was scheduled to host this week, and called off a meeting of the International Big Cat Alliance, an India-led grouping of 95 nations that collaborate in the conservation of seven principal big cat species: tiger, lion, leopard, snow leopard, cheetah, jaguar, and puma. Many of the members of the alliance are from Africa.Thailand has announced that visitors from the DRC and Uganda will only be allowed to enter from Bangkok’s Suvarnabhumi Airport, after testing negative during screening on arrival.On Monday, Mexico’s health secretary also announced increased Ebola screening at airports.Will these measures stop the spread of the virus?The Bundibugyo strain is a rare, highly fatal species of the Ebola virus, which causes severe viral haemorrhagic fever. It spreads through close physical contact with the blood or bodily fluids of an infected or deceased person, as well as via contaminated objects. Therefore, measures limiting contact provide an effective way of containing infections.At the national level in the DRC and Uganda, WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus this week said the response included contact tracing, establishing treatment centres, and infection prevention and control.However, “the delay in detecting the outbreak means that we are now playing catch-up with a very fast-moving epidemic”. “We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment, the epidemic is outpacing us,” he said.“But we know this virus, and we know how to stop it,” Tedros added. “We have stopped every previous Ebola outbreak, and we will stop this one, too.”Is air travel safe?The United Nations has called on airlines and governments to stick strictly to protocols established by the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) during the global COVID-19 pandemic. contentParagraphs.zh: 刚果民主共和国（刚果）和乌干达最近爆发了一种罕见的埃博拉病毒株，促使多国政府采取行动，以阻止该疾病的传播。自金沙萨于 5 月 15 日宣布爆发埃博拉病毒以来，世界卫生组织（WHO）已记录刚果民主共和国有 220 例疑似死亡病例和 900 例致命的本迪布焦（BVD）埃博拉病毒株疑似病例。在乌干达，已报告 5 例病例和 1 例死亡。已确认。推荐故事清单 3 项清单 1（共 3）埃博拉中心的袭击在疫情爆发的恐惧中加剧，刚果民主共和国东部埃博拉中心的袭击加剧清单 2（共 3）埃博拉在刚果民主共和国蔓延，当局努力控制疫情清单 3（共 3）一种罕见的埃博拉病毒株是如何超越医生的？清单结束联合国卫生机构上周将刚果民主共和国的风险评估从“高”提高到“非常高”，但继续将全球风险评估为“低”尽管如此，一些国家已经宣布了旅行禁令和临时边境措施，以遏制新毒株的传播。在受影响国家遏制病毒的努力本周，刚果交通和通讯部暂停了所有往返刚果民主共和国东部布尼亚的航班，试图遏制埃博拉疫情。布尼亚卫生区是刚果民主共和国受该疾病影响的 11 个卫生区之一。一些例外情况，例如人道主义、医疗和紧急航班，在获得航空和卫生当局的特别批准后可能会被允许。乌干达还对往返刚果民主共和国的旅行实施了限制。所有直飞航班均已暂停，巴士和船只过境也已暂停四个星期。边境地区的每周集市已暂停。不过，货运、必需品和食品供应仍被允许过境。实施旅行禁令的国家除了直接受影响的地区外，加拿大和巴哈马表示，将暂时禁止刚果民主共和国、乌干达和南苏丹的居民入境。政府表示，从周三开始，这些国家的居民将在 90 天内无法前往加拿大。加拿大公共卫生机构表示，最近几周去过受影响地区的加拿大公民、永久居民和其他外国人，即使没有表现出症状，也必须从5月30日起隔离21天。巴哈马政府表示，入境限制将立即生效，并持续30天，但须接受加勒比国家卫生部的审查。上周，美国禁止所有在过去一段时间前往刚果民主共和国、乌干达或南苏丹的非公民。入境后21天。周五，美国疾病控制与预防中心 (CDC) 将禁令范围扩大到过去 21 天内去过这些国家的绿卡持有者。 曾前往受影响国家的美国公民已被告知通过选定的配备强化筛查的机场返回美国。这些是华盛顿杜勒斯国际机场 (IAD)，适用于 2026 年 5 月 21 日之后出发飞往美国的航班；亚特兰大哈茨菲尔德-杰克逊国际机场 (ATL) 5 月 22 日之后的航班；以及休斯敦乔治布什洲际机场 (IAH)，适用于 5 月 26 日之后出发的航班。《华尔街日报》报道称，特朗普政府预计将向肯尼亚派遣美国公共卫生官员，为一个潜在的检疫设施配备人员，该设施旨在接待那些在该地区接触过病毒或检测呈阳性的高风险的美国人，以及那些已经检测呈阳性的人。约旦于 5 月 19 日暂停了来自刚果民主共和国和乌干达的人员入境，据约旦国家通讯社报道。同一天，巴林也暂停来自南苏丹、刚果民主共和国和乌干达的外国旅客入境，为期30天。加拿大、巴哈马、美国、约旦和巴林均未出现埃博拉病例。各国加强筛查。除了发布旅行警告，要求本国公民避免前往刚果民主共和国、乌干达和南苏丹外，印度还在主要国际机场设立了额外的筛查措施。新德里还推迟了原定举办的印非峰会本周，并取消了国际大型猫科动物联盟的会议，该联盟由印度领导，由 95 个国家组成，合作保护七种主要大型猫科动物：老虎、狮子、豹子、雪豹、猎豹、美洲虎和美洲狮。该联盟的许多成员都来自非洲。泰国宣布，来自刚果民主共和国和乌干达的游客在抵达时筛查结果呈阴性后，只允许从曼谷素万那普机场入境。周一，墨西哥卫生部长还宣布加强机场埃博拉筛查。这些措施会阻止病毒传播吗？本迪布焦毒株是埃博拉病毒中一种罕见的、高度致命的病毒，会导致严重的病毒性出血热。它通过与感染者或死者的血液或体液的密切身体接触以及通过受污染的物体传播。因此，限制接触的措施提供了遏制感染的有效方法。在刚果民主共和国和乌干达国家层面，世卫组织总干事谭德塞本周表示，应对措施包括追踪接触者、建立治疗中心以及感染预防和控制。然而，“疫情检测的延迟意味着我们现在正在追赶一种非常快速发展的流行病”。他说：“我们正在紧急扩大行动规模，但目前疫情的发展速度已经超过了我们。”谭德塞补充说：“但我们了解这种病毒，也知道如何阻止它。” “我们已经阻止了之前的每一次埃博拉疫情爆发，我们也将阻止这一次。” 航空旅行安全吗？联合国呼吁航空公司和各国政府严格遵守国际民航组织 (ICAO) 在全球 COVID-19 大流行期间制定的协议。这些包括使用

articles.title: Google employee charged with insider trading over Polymarket bets
articles.summary: Michele Spagnuolo allegedly used insider information to profit from bets on people on Google's most-searched list.
articles.titleZh: 谷歌员工因 Polymarket 押注被控内幕交易
articles.summaryZh: 米歇尔·斯帕格努洛 (Michele Spagnuolo) 涉嫌利用内幕信息从对谷歌搜索次数最多的名单上的人物进行投注中获利。

contentParagraphs.en: Michele Spagnuolo allegedly used insider information to profit from bets on people on Google’s most-searched list.A Google software engineer has been charged with fraud by US authorities after allegedly using insider information to win more than $1.2m in bets on the prediction market platform Polymarket.Michele Spagnuolo, an Italian citizen residing in Switzerland, is accused of using confidential information to wager on the results of Google’s annual most-searched list, according to a criminal complaint unsealed on Wednesday.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Iran war’s big winners: Wall Street, weapons firms, AI and green energylist 2 of 3Gamblers make big profits on Iran strikes, raising insider trading concernslist 3 of 3Would you bet on the next disaster? The rise of prediction marketsend of listUS prosecutors accuse Spagnuolo of using an account named “AlphaRaccoon” to make trades on various markets linked to the results of Google’s 2025 Year in Search.The total sum of the bets was approximately $2.75m, according to the complaint, filed in federal court in New York.Among the bets, Spagnuolo successfully predicted that indie pop musician d4vd would top the list for the most-searched for person last year, hours after accessing confidential data at Google, according to prosecutors.Spagnuolo, 36, faces charges of commodities fraud, wire fraud and money laundering.“Today’s charges reinforce a decades-old message: corporate insiders cannot use confidential business information to turn a profit in our markets,” US Attorney for the Southern District of New York Jay Clayton said in a statement.“Insider trading compromises the integrity of our markets, and the American people want this greed-driven conduct investigated and prosecuted,” Clayton added.Bets on Maduro’s captureGoogle said in a statement that it is working with law enforcement and that using confidential information to place bets is a serious breach of company policy.Spagnuolo has been placed on leave, according to a Google spokesperson.A Polymarket spokesperson said the company had worked closely with the US Attorney’s Office on the investigation and that the firm “is the only prediction platform to date whose cooperation has led to insider trading charges in the United States”.“We are committed to maintaining accurate, fair, and transparent markets as well as enforcing our rules and working with our regulators and law enforcement,” the spokesperson added.Last month, a US soldier was charged with using classified military information to place bets on Polymarket regarding the abduction of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro.Prosecutors accuse Gannon Ken Van Dyke, 38, of cashing in on the US operation against Maduro, to the tune of more than $400,000.
contentParagraphs.zh: 米歇尔·斯帕格努洛 (Michele Spagnuolo) 涉嫌利用内幕信息从对谷歌搜索次数最多的名单上的人物的投注中获利。一名谷歌软件工程师因涉嫌利用内幕信息在预测市场平台 Polymarket 上赢得超过 120 万美元的投注而被美国当局指控犯有欺诈罪。根据一份未密封的刑事起诉书，居住在瑞士的意大利公民米歇尔·斯帕格努洛 (Michele Spagnuolo) 被指控使用机密信息对谷歌年度搜索次数最多的名单的结果进行投注周三。推荐故事列表 3 项列表 3 项中的 1 项伊朗战争大赢家：华尔街、武器公司、人工智能和绿色能源列表 3 项中的 2 项赌徒在伊朗袭击中赚取巨额利润，引发内幕交易担忧列表 3 项（共 3 项）您会押注下一场灾难吗？预测市场的崛起美国检察官指控 Spagnuolo 使用名为“AlphaRaccoon”的账户在与谷歌 2025 年搜索结果相关的各个市场上进行交易。根据向纽约联邦法院提交的起诉书，赌注总额约为 275 万美元。在这些赌注中，Spagnuolo 成功预测独立流行音乐家 d4vd 将位列搜索次数最多的人名单中据检察官称，去年，就在访问谷歌机密数据数小时后。36 岁的斯帕格努洛面临商品欺诈、电汇欺诈和洗钱指控。纽约南区美国检察官杰伊·克莱顿 (Jay Clayton) 在一份声明中表示，“今天的指控强化了几十年前的一个信息：企业内部人员不能利用机密商业信息在我们的市场中获利。”“内幕交易损害了我们市场的完整性，美国人民希望对这种贪婪驱动的行为进行调查和调查。克莱顿补充道。押注马杜罗被捕谷歌在一份声明中表示，它正在与执法部门合作，使用机密信息下注是严重违反公司政策的行为。谷歌发言人称，斯帕格努洛已被停职。Polymarket 发言人表示，该公司已与美国检察官办公室密切合作进行调查，该公司“是迄今为止唯一一家因合作导致美国境内内幕交易指控的预测平台”。“我们致力于发言人补充道：“维护准确、公平和透明的市场，执行我们的规则，并与我们的监管机构和执法部门合作。”上个月，一名美国士兵被指控利用机密军事信息在 Polymarket 就委内瑞拉总统尼古拉斯·马杜罗被绑架一事进行投注。检察官指控 38 岁的甘农·肯·范·戴克 (Gannon Ken Van Dyke) 从美国针对马杜罗的行动中获利超过 40 万美元。

articles.title: Aid cuts and climate change drive deadly malaria surge in Zimbabwe
articles.summary: A surge in malaria cases in Zimbabwe is exposing fragile health systems and growing treatment shortages in rural areas.
articles.titleZh: 援助削减和气候变化导致津巴布韦致命的疟疾激增
articles.summaryZh: 津巴布韦疟疾病例激增暴露出脆弱的卫生系统和农村地区日益严重的治疗短缺。

contentParagraphs.en: Harare, Zimbabwe – Precious Mvundura woke up with joint pain, a high fever and a pounding headache on a chilly autumn morning in eastern Zimbabwe.The 37-year-old initially thought it was just the flu. But when the headache persisted for three days, she became worried.Her five-year-old son had also fallen ill and was sweating heavily.In early May, the pair sought help from a village health worker in Chishakwe, a rural farming community outside Zimbabwe’s third-largest city, Mutare. Both tested positive for malaria.“I felt relieved,” Mvundura told Al Jazeera.“From the moment I took that medication, I started getting better.”Her son has also recovered and is back in school.Their ordeal comes as malaria cases and deaths surge across Zimbabwe after US funding cuts disrupted key malaria control programmes.Shortly after returning to office for a second term in 2025, US President Donald Trump slashed foreign aid funding, including programmes backed by the United States Agency for International Development (USAID). In Zimbabwe, the cuts disrupted tuberculosis, HIV/AIDS and malaria research, prevention and treatment programmes.Among the affected initiatives were the Zimbabwe Entomological Support Programme in Malaria (ZENTO) at Africa University in Mutare, which provided scientific research to support the country’s National Malaria Control Programme, and the Zimbabwe Assistance Programme in Malaria II (ZAPIM II), which helped strengthen malaria diagnosis, treatment and prevention in high-burden districts.USAID had disbursed $270m for health and agriculture programmes in Zimbabwe in 2024.Malaria cases jumped to 65,399 between January and April 2026, up from 36,000 recorded during the same period in 2025 and 17,000 in 2024, according to Zimbabwe’s Ministry of Health National Malaria Control Programme weekly surveillance report.Deaths have also risen sharply, reaching 174 between January and April 2026, compared with 85 during the same period last year and 34 in 2024.Mvundura and her son survived because they sought treatment early. In many other cases, the disease has been fatal.Shortages of mosquito nets, test kitsThomas Chuchu, the health programme lead at Save the Children Zimbabwe, said several malaria elimination activities previously supported by ZAPIM II had been disrupted.“In practice, elimination has continued through government and other partners, but with weaker operational capacity and slower implementation,” Chuchu told Al Jazeera.Zimbabwe’s dependence on donor funding for essential medicines, diagnostic kits and mosquito-control supplies has left the country vulnerable [Farai Shawn Matiashe/Al Jazeera]The ZAPIM II programme ran through Zimbabwe’s Ministry of Health system in 11 districts across the provinces of Central and East Mashonaland and the province of Matabeleland North.Before falling ill, Mvundura said she had not been using mosquito nets or repellents.“I only started using a mosquito net a friend shared when I fell sick,” she said.In December 2025, Caroline Mawombedzi was diagnosed with malaria while living in Burma Valley, a farming community about an hour’s drive from Mutare.She had last contracted the disease in the late 2000s while still a child.In mid-May, her five-year-old daughter was also diagnosed with malaria by a village health worker in Chishakwe after suffering severe headaches and stomach problems.Although her daughter received treatment, Mawombedzi said she could not afford preventive measures such as mosquito nets.“I am unemployed. I cannot afford to buy a mosquito net. We have not been sleeping under a mosquito net for years,” she said.Virginia Chakandinakira, a village health worker serving Chishakwe, said malaria diagnostic kits and drugs are now in short supply.“I used to get plenty of malaria test kits and drugs. But in 2025, they did not give me. I referred everyone showing malaria to a nearby Chitakatira clinic,” she said. Chitakatira is a rural settlement about an hour’s drive from Chishakwe.“I only received test kits and drugs in February. However, the supplies are limited. The authorities told us they were only distributing them to hotspot communities.”Research programmes crippledProfessor Sungano Mharakurwa, the director of Africa University’s Malaria Institute, said the abrupt withdrawal of US support had worsened the malaria outbreak by affecting the programme.ZENTO was contributing data from the surveillance of malaria-carrying mosquitoes, which guided strategies employed by the National Malaria Control Programme to control malaria transmission, he said.The Trump administration’s funding cuts have also effectively put a stop to the US President’s Malaria Initiative (PMI), launched in 2005 by former President George W Bush to control and eliminate malaria worldwide. Mharakurwa said the PMI had played a major role in funding malaria medications, and communities had been left exposed without it.He said the Malaria Institute later secured funding from the United Methodist Church General Board of Global Ministry, but it fell far short of previous US assistance.Zimbabwe’s dependence on donor funding for essential medicines, diagnostic kits and mosquito-control supplies has left the country vulnerable.Itai Rusike, the director of Zimbabwe’s Community Working Group on Health, said the government needed to strengthen domestic health financing to reduce dependence on foreign donors.“It is risky for a country to depend substantially on external partners, as donors can withdraw financial support anytime should their interests shift,” he said.Climate change fuels spreadExperts say climate change is also driving the spread of malaria and other vector-borne diseases across Africa.Rising temperatures are allowing malaria to spread into higher-altitude areas, which were once less vulnerable to outbreaks.Zimbabwe experienced El Niño between 2023 and 2024, a climate phenomenon marked by unusually warm temperatures in the Pacific Ocean, which typically disrupts rain
contentParagraphs.zh: 津巴布韦哈拉雷——津巴布韦东部一个寒冷的秋日早晨，普莱西斯·姆文杜拉 (Precious Mvundura) 醒来时感到关节疼痛、高烧和剧烈头痛。这位 37 岁的男子最初以为这只是流感。但当头痛持续了三天后，她开始担心起来。她五岁的儿子也生病了，满头大汗。5月初，两人向津巴布韦第三大城市穆塔雷郊外的农村农业社区奇沙奎的一名乡村卫生工作者寻求帮助。两人的疟疾检测呈阳性。“我感到如释重负，”姆文杜拉告诉半岛电视台。“从我服用这种药物的那一刻起，我的情况就开始好转。”她的儿子也康复了，并回到了学校。美国削减资金扰乱了关键的疟疾控制计划，导致津巴布韦各地的疟疾病例和死亡人数激增。在 2025 年重返总统连任后不久，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普削减了对外援助资金，包括由美国政府支持的项目。美国国际开发署（USAID）。在津巴布韦，削减措施扰乱了结核病、艾滋病毒/艾滋病和疟疾的研究、预防和治疗计划。受影响的举措包括穆塔雷非洲大学的津巴布韦疟疾昆虫学支持计划（ZENTO）和津巴布韦疟疾援助计划二（ZAPIM II），该计划提供科学研究支持该国的国家疟疾控制计划，该计划帮助加强高负担地区的疟疾诊断、治疗和预防2024 年，美国国际开发署为津巴布韦的卫生和农业项目拨款 2.7 亿美元。根据津巴布韦卫生部国家疟疾控制计划每周监测报告，2026 年 1 月至 4 月期间，疟疾病例从 2025 年同期的 36,000 例和 2024 年的 17,000 例跃升至 65,399 例。死亡人数也急剧上升， 2026 年 1 月至 4 月期间，这一数字达到 174 例，而去年同期为 85 例，2024 年为 34 例。Mvundura 和她的儿子因为早期寻求治疗而幸存下来。在许多其他情况下，这种疾病是致命的。缺乏蚊帐、检测试剂盒津巴布韦救助儿童会卫生项目负责人托马斯·楚楚 (Thomas Chuchu) 表示，之前由 ZAPIM II 支持的几项消除疟疾活动已被打乱。“实际上，消除工作是通过政府和其他合作伙伴继续进行的，但行动能力较弱，实施速度较慢。”楚楚告诉半岛电视台。津巴布韦对基本药物、诊断工具包和蚊虫控制用品的捐助资金的依赖已经消失[Farai Shawn Matiashe/半岛电视台]ZAPIM II 计划贯穿津巴布韦卫生部系统，覆盖中马绍纳兰省、东马绍纳兰省以及北马塔贝莱兰省的 11 个地区。姆文杜拉表示，在生病之前，她没有使用蚊帐或驱虫剂。“我只是在生病时才开始使用朋友分享的蚊帐。”2025 年 12 月，卡罗琳Mawombedzi 在缅甸山谷居住时被诊断出患有疟疾，这是一个距穆塔雷约一小时车程的农业社区。她最后一次感染这种疾病是在 2000 年代末，当时还是个孩子。5 月中旬，她 5 岁的女儿在遭受严重头痛和胃病后，也被 Chishakwe 的一名乡村卫生工作者诊断出患有疟疾。尽管她的女儿接受了治疗，但 Mawombedzi 说她买不起蚊帐等预防措施。 “我失业了。我买不起蚊帐。我们已经好几年没有睡在蚊帐里了，”她说。为 Chishakwe 服务的乡村卫生工作者弗吉尼亚·查坎迪纳基拉 (Virginia Chakandinakira) 说，疟疾诊断试剂盒和药物现在供不应求。“我曾经获得了大量的疟疾检测试剂盒和药物。但在 2025 年，我没有将所有显示疟疾的人转介给附近的 Chitakatira 诊所。” Chitakatira 是距离 Chishakwe 约一小时车程的一个农村定居点。“我在 2 月份才收到检测试剂盒和药物。但是，供应有限。当局告诉我们，他们只将它们分发给热点社区。” 研究计划陷入瘫痪 非洲大学疟疾研究所所长 Sungano Mharakurwa 教授表示，美国突然撤回支持，影响了该计划，导致疟疾疫情恶化。ZENTO 正在提供监测数据他说，该计划指导了国家疟疾控制计划采用的控制疟疾传播的策略。特朗普政府的资金削减也有效地阻止了美国总统疟疾倡议（PMI），该倡议由前总统乔治·W·布什于2005年发起，旨在控制和消除全球疟疾。姆哈拉库瓦表示，PMI 在为疟疾药物提供资金方面发挥了重要作用，如果没有它，社区就面临风险。他说，疟疾研究所后来从联合卫理公会全球事工总委员会获得了资金，但远远低于美国之前的援助水平。津巴布韦对基本药物、诊断试剂盒和蚊虫控制用品的捐助资金的依赖使该国很脆弱。津巴布韦社区卫生工作组主任伊泰·鲁西克表示，政府需要加强国内他说：“一个国家严重依赖外部合作伙伴是有风险的，因为如果利益发生变化，捐助者可以随时撤回财政支持。”气候变化助长了疟疾和其他媒介传播疾病在非洲的传播。专家表示，气候变化也正在推动疟疾和其他媒介传播疾病在整个非洲的传播。气温上升使疟疾蔓延到高海拔地区，而这些地区曾经不易爆发疫情。津巴布韦在 2023 年至 2023 年期间经历了厄尔尼诺现象。 2024 年，太平洋出现异常温暖的气候现象，通常会扰乱降雨

articles.title: Parisians defy swimming ban to cool off as record heat hits Europe
articles.summary: Parisians cooled off in the city’s Saint-Martin canal as an unprecedented heatwave pushed temperatures across Europe.
articles.titleZh: 欧洲创纪录高温，巴黎人无视游泳禁令降温
articles.summaryZh: 由于前所未有的热浪使整个欧洲气温升高，巴黎人在圣马丁运河里避暑。

contentParagraphs.en: NewsFeedParisians cooled off in the city’s Saint-Martin canal as an unprecedented heatwave pushed temperatures across Europe far above seasonal norms. Swimmers ignored long-standing bans, swimming outside designated bathing sites.Published On 28 May 2026
contentParagraphs.zh: 由于前所未有的热浪使欧洲各地的气温远高于季节性正常水平，巴黎人在该市的圣马丁运河中降温。游泳者无视长期禁令，在指定沐浴地点外游泳。发布于 2026 年 5 月 28 日
